IB chemistry study tool

VSEPR Studio

Generate a quick molecular geometry diagram from the steric number and lone-pair count, or jump in with a preset molecule. The diagram, shape name, and bond-angle notes update live, which makes it useful for explanation, retrieval, and screen-shared teaching.

VSEPR Geometry generator Local and shareable
Presets by VSEPR pattern

Use the presets or the X/E controls below the diagram to choose a supported VSEPR pattern.

Molecular geometry: trigonal pyramidal.

Four regions of electron density arrange tetrahedrally. One of those regions is a lone pair, so the visible atoms form a trigonal pyramidal shape.
